title: Klein-Gordon as the Linear Phase-Field Equation
method: WKB / eikonal expansion from causal wave equation with conformal d'Alembertian
causal_dalembertian: Box_tilde = A^{-2} Box_M + 2 A^{-3} eta^{mu nu} (d_mu A)(d_nu)
key_result: KG operator recovered as linear equation whose eikonal limit is g-tilde-HJ

orders_in_hbar.O_hbar_minus_2: g-tilde-Hamilton-Jacobi equation: (d_t S)^2 - |grad S|^2 = A^2 m^2 c^2
orders_in_hbar.O_hbar_0: Quantum potential + shear-amplitude coupling: A^{-2} Box_M R + 2 A^{-3} eta(dA, dR)

screened_limit: A->1, d_mu A->0 reduces to standard Klein-Gordon
contrast_with_standard: Standard QM postulates operator substitution E->i hbar d_t; TEP recovers KG as the linear wave equation in the causal metric, whose eikonal limit is the geometric HJ equation.
mass_convention: Bare parameter m in action S = -m c^2 integral d_tau_tilde; local frequency omega_local = m c^2 A(phi) / hbar; effective inertial mass m_eff -> m in screened limit.
